logs.title: 72-point checklist: 71 pass, 1 fail — Item 23: TP-008 calibration expired
reasoning: Cat A-B (Identity, Dispensing, Items 1-14): All PASS — MBR v09 correct, all 9 materials within ±0.5%, HPMC viscosity 98,400 mPa·s in range
Cat C (Equipment Calibration, Items 15-24): FAIL on Item 23 — TP-008 calibration due 10-Mar-2024, used 15-Mar-2024, expired 5 days; all other 9 equipment items current
Cat D-F (Line Clearance, CPPs, IPC, Items 25-48): All PASS — granulation torque 10.3 Nm, LOD 1.9%, blend RSD 0.06%, all 20 IPC compression checks in spec
Cat G-H (Yields, QC, Items 49-62): All PASS — overall yield 98.9%, assay 100.4%, dissolution S1 PASS all 5 timepoints, AV 4.2
Cat I-J (Items 63-72): All PASS — ALCOA+ met, LIMS ΔT ≤1 min throughout, API accountability 100.24%, all 3 QAIP witnesses present

logs.title: 1 CRITICAL finding: TP-008 expired calibration — Confidence: 62% — HOLD
reasoning: Primary finding: TP-008 calibration certificate CAL-TP008-2023-0089 due 10-Mar-2024; compression performed 15-Mar-2024 — 5 days post-expiry
Finding type: equipment_calibration. Severity: CRITICAL per MBR Section 3.1 and Section 10.1
Regulatory reference: 21 CFR 211.68, 21 CFR 211.105; MBR Step 7.1 pre-check, Checklist Item 23
Evidence: EBR Step 7.1 — due date 10-Mar-2024 correctly recorded, but "Within calibration? Y" marked incorrectly by JW; countersigned by TO; no deviation raised
Confidence: 97% base − 15 (critical) − 20 (no deviation raised modifier) = 62% — mandatory full human review

logs.title: QA escalation drafted — awaiting send approval
reasoning: TP-008 (Tablet Press, semi-annual calibration) used 5 days past calibration expiry on 15-Mar-2024
Operator JW recorded "Within calibration? Y" — incorrect; constitutes a data integrity error per 21 CFR 211.68
MBR Step 7.1 MANDATORY PRE-CHECK requires STOP and deviation if calibration expired — no deviation raised
QAIP countersigned without independent verification of calibration certificate
Out-of-calibration impact assessment required: were compression force readings reliable during the run?

artifacts.label: QA Escalation — Equipment Calibration Violation
artifacts.subject: HOLD: Critical Equipment Calibration Finding — Batch MET-2024-0042 Metformin 500mg ER
artifacts.body:
Dear QA Head,

Batch MET-2024-0042 (Metformin HCl ER 500mg Tablets) has been placed on HOLD.

CRITICAL FINDING — Equipment Calibration Expired:
TP-008 (Tablet Press) semi-annual calibration certificate CAL-TP008-2023-0089 expired 10-Mar-2024. Compression performed 15-Mar-2024 — 5 days post-expiry. Operator JW recorded "Within calibration? Y" which is incorrect.

MBR Section 3.1 classifies this as CRITICAL. 21 CFR 211.68 and 211.105 require equipment used in manufacturing to have current calibration.

Required actions:
1. Raise critical deviation per SOP-DEV-001
2. Schedule TP-008 recalibration immediately
3. Perform out-of-calibration impact assessment on compression force data
4. Review historical TP-008 calibration performance

Batch remains on HOLD until TP-008 recalibration confirmed and QA disposition complete.

Regards,
Pace — Batch Record Review
Lilly Quality Operations
